name: Toggle/Hold Option
description: A setting that lets sustained actions use hold, toggle, or both input behavior.
design_notes: Toggle/hold options reduce sustained input strain and make common actions adaptable. Each supported action needs reset rules so toggle state does not persist into unsafe contexts.
design_purpose: Make sustained actions usable for more players.
Respect player preference for aim, crouch, sprint, block, or interact actions.
Reduce accidental fatigue from long holds.
player_fantasy: The player feels the control scheme adapts to their body and device rather than forcing one input style.

parameters.name: toggle_reset_contexts
parameters.description: Contexts that clear active toggle state.

edge_cases: Toggle aim or crouch should clear on death, respawn, cutscene, and sometimes weapon swap.
Menus must not accidentally activate gameplay toggles.
Cloud saves and local profiles can disagree on preferences.
common_bugs: Changing setting while action is held leaves the action stuck active.
Toggle state is saved as if it were a preference.
Prompt text says hold while behavior is toggle.
balancing_notes: Accessibility input options should not be treated as difficulty cheats unless the action itself changes gameplay timing.
Hybrid modes need careful debounce windows to avoid accidental toggles.
accessibility_notes: This mechanic is itself an accessibility affordance and should be available early in settings, not hidden behind gameplay progress.

implementation_notes.unity: Put hold/toggle conversion in the input abstraction layer so gameplay code reads action state, while per-action preferences and reset contexts live in the player profile.
implementation_notes.godot: Store input-mode preferences in a settings Resource and resolve action active state through helper functions that can clear toggles on death, menus, or context changes.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Enhanced Input triggers or a gameplay input component to apply per-action hold/toggle behavior before abilities or movement code consume the action.
implementation_notes.web: Convert keyboard, mouse, touch, and gamepad events into normalized action states using profile preferences, then clear toggles from configured reset contexts.

example_games.title: The Last of Us Part II
example_games.note: Includes accessibility settings that let players adjust hold and toggle behavior.
